---
title: "The OpenPipeline ecosystem"
description: "One core package, four satellites, all built the same way."
order: 10
---

OpenPipeline is not a single monolith. It is a **core package** plus a set of
**satellite packages** that extend it for specific domains and hardware. All are
[Viash](viash.qmd) packages built the same way, so the same components,
data format, and tooling carry across every one of them.

The core defines the whole single-cell flow; each satellite pins the core as a
dependency and adds to it rather than forking it.

| Package | Role |
|---|---|
| [`openpipeline`]{.pkg-tag .pkg-openpipeline}<span class="swatch openpipeline"></span> | Core single-cell multi-omics — RNA, protein/ADT, ATAC, VDJ, GDO |
| [`openpipeline_spatial`]{.pkg-tag .pkg-openpipeline_spatial}<span class="swatch openpipeline_spatial"></span> | Spatial transcriptomics — ingests Visium, Visium HD, Xenium, Aviti24, CosMx (or any pre-ingested count matrix) |
| [`openpipeline_qc`]{.pkg-tag .pkg-openpipeline_qc}<span class="swatch openpipeline_qc"></span> | Quality-control components and standalone reporting |
| [`openpipeline_rapids`]{.pkg-tag .pkg-openpipeline_rapids}<span class="swatch openpipeline_rapids"></span> | GPU-accelerated drop-ins via rapids-singlecell — *coming soon* |
| [`openpipeline_composed`]{.pkg-tag .pkg-openpipeline_composed}<span class="swatch openpipeline_composed"></span> | Turnkey composed pipelines that wrap the whole flow |

## How the packages relate

Every package plugs into the same [pipeline architecture](architecture.qmd):

- **`openpipeline`** defines all stages — ingestion, QC, processing, integration,
  and downstream — for the RNA, protein, ATAC, VDJ, and GDO modalities.
- **`openpipeline_spatial`** brings spatial transcriptomics. It currently ingests
  Visium, Visium HD, Xenium, Aviti24, and CosMx — or you start from an
  already-ingested count matrix of any technology and run the spatial processing
  workflow.
- **`openpipeline_qc`** slots quality-control reporting alongside ingestion,
  producing a standalone HTML report before you commit to filter thresholds.
- **`openpipeline_rapids`** (*coming soon*) will provide GPU drop-in
  replacements for the compute-heavy processing and integration steps.
- **`openpipeline_composed`** bundles the stages into end-to-end pipelines, so a
  whole cohort goes from raw data to an annotated object in one run.

Because a satellite only *pins* the core, it inherits the core's components and
[MuData](mudata.qmd) format automatically — and stays compatible as the core
evolves. This is [modularity](modularity.qmd) applied at the package level.

:::{.callout-note appearance="simple"}
`biobox` and `craftbox` are external component libraries that some packages
depend on. They are dependencies, not OpenPipeline pipelines — you may see them
referenced, but they are documented in their own projects.
:::

Browse every component and workflow across all packages in the
[reference](../reference/index.qmd).
